name: gitreport
description: Generate a Git repository report with commit heatmap, contributor stats, and trend analysis

steps.name: Fetch Git Repository Data
steps.prompt:
Fetch git commit history and contributor data from the current Git repository. 
        Use 'git log' commands (or equivalent Node.js git library) to extract:
        - All commits with author, date, message, file changes
        - Contributor names and commit counts
        - Daily/weekly commit frequency
        Output a JSON file at /artifacts/gitreport/git-data.json with structure:
        { commits: [{author, date, message, filesChanged}, ...], contributors: [{name, count}, ...], dailyStats: [...] }

steps.name: Generate Complete HTML Report with Embedded Charts
steps.prompt:
Read the git data from /artifacts/gitreport/git-data.json and generate a complete, self-contained HTML file.
        The HTML must include:
        1. A commit heatmap (calendar-style grid or chart showing commits per day/week)
        2. A contributor table with names, commit counts, and percentages
        3. Trend visualization (commits over time)
        4. Summary statistics (total commits, total contributors, date range)
        5. All CSS styling EMBEDDED in <style> tags
        6. All JavaScript (chart rendering, data processing) EMBEDDED in <script> tags
        7. Use a lightweight charting library like Chart.js or canvas-based visualization
        Output a complete, valid HTML file at /artifacts/gitreport/report.html that opens in a browser.
        Do NOT create separate .css or .js files — everything must be in the single HTML file.

steps.name: Verify Report Generation
steps.prompt:
Verify that /artifacts/gitreport/report.html was created successfully and is valid HTML.
        Check that:
        1. The file exists and is not empty
        2. It contains opening and closing <html> tags
        3. It includes embedded <style> and <script> tags
        4. It has the heatmap, contributor table, and trend sections
        Print a success message showing the output path: /artifacts/gitreport/report.html
        If any checks fail, provide detailed error information.
